rationale: Both candidates correctly validate the core runner fix and the qwen3_tts gating, and both raise a similar 'async_chunk default True vs False inconsistency' point that echoes the ground-truth Low concern. X is more explicit and precise: it directly confirms the Med-comment scoping is safe with concrete evidence, and its second non-blocking note explicitly identifies that the split now only handles the scheduled-tokens axis and drops the old hidden-shape axis, correctly forecasting the dual-batch-axis fix later shipped in #4910 — a clean gap_hit. Y's parallel finding (assertion for shape mismatch in to_payload_element) is grounded but more generic and doesn't name the two-axis distinction, and Y spends one finding on an off-topic style nit (extracting a qwen3_tts helper) that dilutes precision. Neither addresses amy's model-placement question, capping recall for both.
